framework.name: Stateful vs Stateless Auth Trade-off
framework.description: Server-side sessions (stateful) provide better control and revocability but create database bottlenecks in distributed systems. Token-based auth (stateless) scales horizontally but sacrifices immediate revocation capability. For agent authorization, this trade-off may determine whether systems can enforce real-time spending limits.
framework.raw_quote: you'll need to store the session id in a database or keep it in memory on the server because most of today's cloud applications are scaled horizontally this can be a huge bottleneck
